reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes all hypotheses: `DifferentiableOn ℂ f (ball 0 1)` is equivalent to "holomorphic on the unit disc" (via `DifferentiableOn.analyticOnNhd` on open sets in ℂ), `Bornology.IsBounded (f '' (ball 0 1))` correctly expresses "bounded on the disc," and `¬ Set.EqOn f 0 (ball 0 1)` correctly says "not identically zero on the disc." The conclusion sums over the subtype of distinct zeros in the ball, weighted by `(analyticOrderAt f z).toNat`, which is the standard way to convert ∑(1−|zₙ|) "with multiplicity" (as meant in Shakarchi) into a sum over a set: since f is analytic and not identically zero on the connected ball, `analyticOrderAt f z ≠ ⊤` at every zero by the identity theorem, so `.toNat` yields the correct finite multiplicity. Summability over the subtype matches the informal convergence claim. No hypotheses are dropped or strengthened.
